title: These are the 28 victims killed in Iranian missile attacks during the 12-day conflict
author_or_org: Amy Spiro / The Times of Israel
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 104-107 in tool view
strengths: Named-victim roundup for the full 12-day window; explicitly states that 27 were civilians and one was an off-duty soldier; also reports aggregate missile, injury, and displacement context.
limitations: Media summary rather than official casualty database; does not itself provide denominator data.
notes: Useful primary press compilation for the full 12-day numerator and for the status sensitivity around the off-duty soldier.

citation_short: Times of Israel, 27 Jun 2025 (war by numbers)
title: The Israel-Iran war by the numbers after 12 days of fighting
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 109-126 in tool view
strengths: Independent aggregate summary for the same 12-day window; reiterates that all but one of the dead were civilians and reports hospital-treated casualties.
limitations: Journalistic aggregation; casualty counts are not disaggregated into a full official status ledger.
notes: Used mainly as corroboration for the numerator and the status composition.

title: Authorities identify three of four killed in Iranian missile attack on Beersheba
pages_or_sections: title and main text; lines 69-80 and line 126 in tool views
strengths: Explicitly identifies Cpl. Eitan Zacks as an off-duty soldier and notes that apart from him, all those killed in the 12-day conflict were civilians.
limitations: Single-incident reporting; still a media source.
notes: This is the clearest source for the classification sensitivity that governs whether a publishable DR can be kept.

title: The announcement by the Chief of the General Staff, LTG Eyal Zamir
pages_or_sections: main text; line 44 in tool view
strengths: Official postwar statement explicitly confirming that the IDF called up 'tens of thousands of soldiers'.
limitations: Does not disclose the exact call-up figure or in-country deployment composition.
notes: Used to ground the reserve-call-up scenario range rather than to claim a precise troop count.

title: Home Front Command forces both regular and reservists are deployed and operating at the impact sites, participating in the rescue and recovery efforts
pages_or_sections: title and main text; lines 28-36 in tool view
strengths: Official confirmation that both regulars and reservists were operating at impact sites inside Israel.
limitations: No headcount and no breakdown by service branch.
notes: Supports the in-country military-exposure framing for the 12-day episode.

title: Operation Rising Lion: A Special Home Front Situation
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 114-122 in tool view
strengths: Explains that a 'special home front situation' was declared with the launch of Operation Rising Lion and describes the measure as applying when civilian population is likely to be attacked in all or part of Israel.
limitations: Legal-policy explanation rather than population measurement.
notes: Used to justify treating civilian exposure as statewide rather than trying to model only struck municipalities.

title: Israeli population tops 10 million for 1st time
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 90-93 in tool view
strengths: Reports Israel Central Bureau of Statistics figure of almost 10.1 million population shortly before the war.
limitations: Press report of a CBS release rather than the release itself.
notes: Used as the baseline population stock for the civilian denominator.

title: Israel embarks on plan to bring more than 100,000 Israelis stuck abroad safely home
author_or_org: Sharon Wrobel / The Times of Israel
pages_or_sections: main text; line 102 in tool view
strengths: Provides a wartime upper-bound context for temporary civilian absence from the country during the bombardment window.
limitations: Not a daily stock series; stranded-abroad figure includes people attempting to return and not all were absent for the full 12 days.
notes: Used only to bound the civilian-exposure adjustment, not as an exact subtraction.

title: Israel plans Gaza conquest, but how strong is its army?
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 79-81 in tool view
strengths: Reports IISS Military Balance figures of 169,500 active personnel and 465,000 reservists shortly before the war.
limitations: Secondary reporting rather than direct use of IISS tables; not specific to the June air war.
notes: Used as the baseline active-duty stock and reserve pool ceiling for the in-country military denominator scenario.
